message:
fix(wal,core,pager): prevent ABA hazard in WAL page index by tracking generation identity

The cached page index was invalidated based solely on WAL salts, which
allowed an ABA hazard when a checkpoint reset reused the same salt pair
with a new checkpoint sequence number. This could cause stale index
entries from a previous generation to shadow pages in the new generation.

Introduce WalGenerationIdentity (checkpoint_seq + salts) as the
canonical generation discriminant. The WAL adapter now invalidates its
page index when either component changes. The WAL refresh path also
checks checkpoint_seq to detect same-salt resets.

Additionally, replace the inline read_page match arms with an explicit
WalPageLookupResolution enum that distinguishes authoritative hits/misses
from partial-index fallback paths, improving tracing diagnostics and
making the lookup contract self-documenting.

Update WalBackend::read_page doc contract to reflect the authoritative
lookup expectation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
